login.error_generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.
login.reason_session_expired: Your session has expired. Please sign in again.
login.reset_success_banner: Password updated. You can now sign in with your new password.
login.two_factor_app_prompt: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app.
login.two_factor_email_prompt: We sent a 6-digit code to your email. Enter it below.

recovery.title: Lost access to your two-factor authentication?
recovery.body: Enter your account email. If two-factor is enabled on this account, we'll send you a link to disable it.
recovery.success: If the email exists and has 2FA, a disable link is on its way.

disable.title: Disable two-factor authentication
disable.warning: Clicking the button below will permanently remove the second factor from this account. You'll be able to sign in with just your password until you re-enable 2FA.
disable.warning_attacker: If you did NOT request this, close this page and change your password immediately.
disable.success: Two-factor authentication is now disabled. You may sign in.
disable.error_consumed: This link has already been used.
disable.error_expired: This link has expired or is invalid.

password_reset.request_body: Enter your email and we'll send a reset link.
password_reset.request_success: If the email exists, a reset link is on its way.
password_reset.request_success_manual: Email delivery is not configured on this instance. Contact your administrator to obtain a reset link.
password_reset.success_redirect: Password updated. You can now sign in with your new password.
password_reset.link_expired: This link has expired or has already been used.
password_reset.too_short: Password must be at least 8 characters.
password_reset.missing_token: No reset token in the URL.

verification_sent.title: Verify your email
verification_sent.body: We sent a verification link to {{email}}. Click the link to activate your account.
verification_sent.error: Could not resend. Try again later.

verify.success: Email verified. You can now sign in.
verify.expired: This link has expired or is invalid.

register.title: Create account
register.subtitle: Join the research project
register.success_desc: Your account has been created and linked to the study.
register.success_hint: You can now log in to the administrative dashboard to begin collaborating.
register.invalid_desc: This invitation link is invalid or has expired.
register.missing_token_desc: This page requires a valid invitation token. Please check the link you received.
register.privacy_hint: By registering, you agree to follow the research ethics guidelines configured for this study.

capability_banner.smtp: Email delivery is not configured. Account recovery (password reset, email change, email-based two-factor authentication) requires manual administrator action.
capability_banner.s3: Object storage is not configured. Audio responses cannot be collected; audio-enabled studies fall back to text-only responses.
capability_banner.chip_tooltip: Some platform capabilities are unavailable. Click for details.

hub.title: Researcher Hub
hub.no_projects: No projects yet. Create your first project to get started.
hub.no_projects_body: Create a project before adding concourses, Q-sets, or studies.

memo.summary_empty_concourse: How and why you built this concourse
memo.comment_placeholder: Write a comment. Use @ to mention.
memo.save_error: Could not save memo. Try again.
memo.load_error: Could not load memo. Refresh the page to retry.
memo.delete_entry_confirm: Delete this entry and all its comments?

distribution_mode.title: Distribution mode
distribution_mode.helper: Choose how strictly the Q-sort grid is enforced. Forced: each column must be filled to its declared capacity (the most common choice). Free: any number of cards per column, total = Q-set size. Flexible: total enforced, columns are soft hints.

forced.label: Forced
forced.tooltip: Each column must hold exactly its declared capacity.

free.label: Free
free.tooltip: Total cards = Q-set size; per-column count is unconstrained.

flexible.label: Flexible
flexible.tooltip: Total cards = Q-set size; per-column capacities are soft hints (warnings only).

rough_sort.lock_banner: Toggle locked. {{count}} participant(s) have started the survey; archive or delete those sessions before changing this setting.
rough_sort.deck_mode_note: Disabled. Participants see the full Q-set as a horizontally-scrollable deck.

study.activate_error: Could not activate study. Verify the design is valid and try again.

save.synced_warnings: Synced with server. Kept your changes in: {{fields}}
save.synced_concurrent: Synced with concurrent changes from another user
save.conflict: Conflict detected. Some changes could not be merged.

missing.title: Missing statements
missing.desc: Are there any ideas or perspectives that you felt were missing from the set of statements?

account.title: Account settings
account.description: Manage your personal information and account security.

personal.title: Personal information
personal.email_locked: Email changes via the API are coming soon to this page; backend support is live.
personal.name: Full name

security.title: Security
security.description: Manage two-factor authentication and your password.
security.scan_desc: Scan this QR code with your authenticator app (google authenticator, authy, etc.)
security.channel_select_label: How should we deliver your 2FA codes?
security.channel_email_desc: We'll email you a 6-digit code each time you log in. Useful if you can't install an authenticator app.

password.title: Password management
password.error: Failed to change password. Check current password.

sidebar.select_study_msg: Please select or create a study to begin.

concourse.title: Concourse
concourse.description: Manage candidate statements for your Q-methodology study
concourse.create_dialog_desc: A concourse is a collection of candidate statements from which you will select your Q-set.
concourse.empty_desc: Create a concourse to start collecting and organizing candidate statements for your Q-methodology research.
concourse.import_desc: Paste statements separated by newlines. Each line becomes one item.
concourse.no_items: No items yet. Add your first statement.
concourse.delete_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete this concourse and all its items?
concourse.delete_item_confirm: Delete this item? Cannot be undone.
concourse.comment_error: Could not add comment. Try again.
concourse.item_detail_desc: History and comments for this item
concourse.no_tags: No tags yet. Create your first tag above.
concourse.add_language_desc: Enter a language code (e.g. en, fr, fi).
concourse.add_language_desc_v2: Select a language for statement translations.

methodological_context.title: Methodological context
methodological_context.subtitle: Optional documentation fields. Fill in what's relevant to your design; leave others blank.

concourse.import_csv_hint: CSV/TSV needs columns: code, language, text.
concourse.import_csv_no_match: No rows match the selected language ({{lang}}).

concourse_import.title: Import from Concourse
concourse_import.desc: Select items from a concourse to import as study statements. Items are copied; changes to the concourse will not affect the study.
concourse_import.replace_warning: All existing statements will be permanently deleted and replaced by the selected items.
concourse_import.error: Could not import items from the concourse. Try again.
concourse_import.no_accepted: No accepted items in this concourse. Uncheck "Only accepted" to see all.
concourse_import.no_concourses: No concourses in this project. Create one first.

concourse_sync.source_deleted_tip: The source concourse item has been deleted.
concourse_sync.error: Could not sync from the concourse. Try again.
concourse_sync.stale_banner: {{count}} statement(s) have updates available from the concourse.

analysis.title: Analysis
analysis.description: Factor analysis of Q-sort data: extract viewpoints from participant responses
analysis.configuration_description: Select extraction method, number of factors, and rotation
analysis.run_will_replace: Running again will replace the current results view. Use the history panel to compare runs.
analysis.scree_hint: Each bar shows how much variance a factor explains (its eigenvalue). The dashed line marks eigenvalue = 1 (Kaiser criterion). Click a bar to select the number of factors to extract.
analysis.too_few_participants: Need at least 2 completed participants to run analysis.
analysis.eigenvalue_error: Failed to load analysis data. Please try again.
analysis.scree_plot_label: Scree plot showing eigenvalues for {{n}} factors
analysis.empty_state: Set parameters and run the analysis.

empty.contract_body: Q-methodology factor analysis requires at least 2 participants who have completed the sort. Configuration choices (extraction, factors, rotation, flagging) carry meaning once data exists. Share the study link from the overview to start collecting responses.

analysis.caption_statements: Statement z-scores and factor arrays per factor
analysis.manual_flag_hint: Click a loading cell to flag/unflag a participant for that factor. Re-run analysis to update results.
analysis.no_flagged_participants: No participants flagged for any factor. Try adjusting the number of factors or flagging method.
analysis.no_statement_scores: No statement scores available. Ensure participants are flagged for at least one factor.
analysis.loadings_help: Factor loadings show how strongly each participant's sort correlates with each factor. Highlighted cells exceed the significance threshold shown above.
analysis.factor_array_help: Composite Q-sort showing the idealized sort pattern for this factor. Statements are placed in the distribution based on their weighted z-scores.
analysis.statements_help: Z-scores show each factor's standardized position on each statement. D = distinguishing (significantly different between factors), C = consensus (no significant differences).
analysis.z_scores_description: Z-scores and factor array positions per statement
analysis.characteristics_help: Eigenvalues reflect the amount of variance explained. Composite reliability reflects internal consistency among flagged sorts. SE of factor scores reflects the precision of the composite estimate.
analysis.help_extraction: PCA maximises explained variance. Centroid is less mathematically constrained.
analysis.help_n_factors: Each factor represents a distinct viewpoint.
analysis.help_rotation: Varimax separates factors for simpler structure.
analysis.help_flagging: Auto: flag participants loading on exactly one factor. Manual: override per participant.
analysis.guide_loadings_1: Each loading is a correlation (-1 to +1) between a participant and a factor (shared viewpoint).
analysis.guide_loadings_2: Highlighted values exceed the significance threshold shown above the table.
analysis.guide_arrays_1: Each factor array is the composite Q-sort representing one shared viewpoint.
analysis.guide_arrays_2: Read left-to-right as most disagreed to most agreed. Extreme positions carry the strongest signal for interpretation.
analysis.guide_statements_1: Z-scores show how strongly each factor agrees or disagrees with each statement (0 = neutral).
analysis.guide_statements_2: D = distinguishing (placed significantly differently across factors). Stars indicate significance level.
analysis.guide_summary_1: Eigenvalues measure how much variance a factor explains. The Kaiser criterion (eigenvalue > 1) is one common heuristic for deciding how many factors to retain.
analysis.guide_summary_2: Composite reliability reflects how consistently the flagged sorts define each factor. Higher values mean the factor estimate is based on more agreement among its defining sorts.

history.title: Analysis history
history.empty: No previous analyses for this study yet. Run one to start the audit trail.
history.empty_explainer: Each run is logged here so you can document and revisit every decision.
history.load_error: Could not load this analysis run. Refresh the page to retry.
history.notes_placeholder: Add a note about this run…
history.notes_error: Could not save notes. Check your connection and try again.
history.delete_dialog_description: Deleting an analysis run removes evidence of an analytical choice from the audit trail. Are you sure?

factor_voices.title: Voices on Factor {{n}}
factor_voices.no_audio: No post-sort audio recordings from participants flagged on this factor.
factor_voices.no_material: No post-sort audio recordings or written comments from participants flagged on this factor.
factor_voices.error: Could not load audio recordings. Please try again.
factor_voices.context: Ground factor interpretation in participants' own words: their audio rationales and their written card comments.

factor_note.label: Factor narrative
factor_note.placeholder: Write the interpretive narrative for this factor: the discourse, voices, and tensions it foregrounds.
factor_note.empty_hint: Add an interpretive narrative for this factor
factor_note.error: Failed to save the factor narrative.

judgmental.label: Judgmental (manual)
judgmental.tooltip: Manually rotate factor pairs by chosen angles to align factors with substantively-meaningful positions.

manual_rotations.title: Manual rotations
manual_rotations.helper: Specify rotations as 'rotate factor F by Δ° around factor G'. Rotations are applied in order.
manual_rotations.empty: Add at least one rotation to run the analysis.

bootstrap.helper: Optional. Resamples Q-sorts B times to estimate confidence intervals on factor scores.
bootstrap.tooltip: Non-parametric bootstrap of Q-sorts (resampled with replacement); analysis is repeated B times to estimate 95% CIs on z-scores.

explore.advisory: Advisory only. Q-method retention also depends on interpretability and stability (Watts & Stenner 2012).
explore.preview_range_disabled: Preview range supports PCA + varimax only. Centroid extraction and judgmental rotation are path-dependent; commit a real run to inspect.

create.title: Create project
create.url_description: This becomes the project path under /app/.

recruitment.title: Access
recruitment.description: Configure the study URL, manage participant access, and track recruitment efficiency.

study_url.title: Study URL
study_url.description: The public address where participants access your study.
study_url.slug_description: The unique identifier used in the study URL. Lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only.
study_url.slug_locked: The URL slug can only be changed while the study is in draft mode.

recruitment.state_draft: Draft mode. Links will work once you activate the study.
recruitment.state_closed: This study is closed. Existing links remain visible but new participants cannot start.
recruitment.state_archived: This study is archived. All recruitment data is read-only.
recruitment.empty_description: Create your first link to start inviting participants.

access_rules.title: Access rules
access_rules.description: Control when and how participants can access the study.
access_rules.password_toggle_desc: Participants must enter a password before starting the study.
access_rules.password_locked: Password protection can only be changed while the study is in draft mode.
access_rules.password_set: A password is currently set. Enter a new value to change it, or toggle off to remove.
access_rules.window_toggle_help: Restrict participant access to a specific time range.
access_rules.date_hint: Leave empty for no time restriction.
access_rules.non_draft_notice: Outside draft, only the collection window can be edited. Switch the study back to draft to change other access rules.
access_rules.archived_notice: This study is archived. Access rules are read-only.

recruitment.name_placeholder: E.g. LinkedIn, email batch a, student list...
recruitment.share_desc: Distribute your study URL to invite participants.
recruitment.qr_print_hint: Scan or print this code for physical recruitment materials (flyers, posters).
recruitment.analytics_desc: Direct links and QR scans are automatically tracked in the health dashboard above.

guidance.public: Best for high-volume recruitment. A single URL for all participants.
guidance.individual: Maximum security. Generates unique links that expire after one successful completion.
guidance.limited: Controlled access. One link that stops working after a fixed number of submissions.

table.name: Campaign / lot
table.type_help: Public, Single-use, or Capacity-limited. See strategy descriptions in the "New access link" dialog.

toasts.failed: Could not create recruitment links. Check the inputs and try again.
toasts.revoke_failed: Could not revoke this link. It may already be in use.

dashboard.title: Project dashboard
dashboard.snapshot: Here's a snapshot of your research activity.
dashboard.no_studies: No studies found. Create your first study to get started!
dashboard.get_started: Get started with your research project
dashboard.step_concourse_desc: Add the candidate statements from your literature review.
dashboard.step_qset_desc: Review and accept the items that will form your Q-set.
dashboard.step_study_desc: Define the sorting grid for your Q-sort.
dashboard.step_import: Import the Q-set into your study
dashboard.step_import_desc: Import the accepted concourse items as study statements.
dashboard.step_configure_desc: Set up instructions, presort, and postsort questions.
dashboard.step_launch_desc: Generate participation links and share them.
dashboard.concourse_empty: No items yet. Start building your statement collection.
dashboard.n_studies_help: Total number of studies in this project, including drafts and closed.
dashboard.n_active_help: Studies currently accepting participant submissions (state = active).
dashboard.n_participants_total_help: Sum of completed participants across all studies in this project.

tool_help.design: Configure the study: distribution grid, conditions of instruction, statements, consent.
tool_help.recruit: Recruitment links, access rules, and study URL settings.
tool_help.data: Participant responses and exports (CSV, Q-sort dumps).
tool_help.analysis: Factor analysis configuration and historical runs.

export.config_error: Could not export configuration. Try again.
export.label: Export data

package_dialog.title: Download research package
package_dialog.description: Bundles all study data into a single ZIP for OSF / pre-registration.
package_dialog.discussion_hint: Adds memo/memo-discussion.md with all comment threads (signed and dated). Off by default; keep your export clean unless you want the deliberation trail.

import.title: Import study configuration
import.upload_desc: Upload a previously exported study configuration file
import.validate_desc: Review configuration and provide a unique slug
import.drag_drop: Drag & drop JSON file or click to browse
import.slug_help: Must be unique, 3-100 characters, lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only
import.failed: Could not import study. See details below and retry.

errors.invalid_grid_config: Invalid grid_config: must be a list

warnings.grid_capacity_mismatch: Statement count ({{count}}) doesn't match grid capacity ({{capacity}})
warnings.missing_consent_draft: Consent title or description missing (draft study)
warnings.external_branding_logo: Branding logo uses an external URL: {{url}}
warnings.external_partner_logo: Partner logo for '{{name}}' uses an external URL: {{url}}
warnings.external_logo: Logo URL references external resource - may not be accessible

create_study.title: Create new study
create_study.description: Start a new q-methodology study. You can configure statements and settings later.
create_study.languages_desc: Select the languages to enable. Content will be initialized with localized defaults.

validation.slug_min: Slug must be at least 3 characters
validation.slug_regex: Slug must contain only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens

study_overview.title: Overview
study_overview.view_all: View all participants and data details

draft.title: Revert to draft?
draft.desc: This will stop data collection but allow you to modify the study design. Existing data is preserved.

active.title: Launch study?
active.desc: Activating the study will open it to participants.

paused.title: Pause study?
paused.desc: Participants will not be able to enter the study while it is paused. You can resume later.

closed.title: Close study?
closed.desc: This will prevent new participants from entering. Existing sessions can still finish. You can reopen later.

notifications.error: Could not change study state. Check your permissions and try again.

design.title: Study design
design.editing_language_banner: Editing the {{language}} version. Use the language selector in the toolbar to switch.

guidance.title: Design guidance
guidance.intro_desc: Start by defining the purpose of your study. This information will be shown to participants before they begin the sorting process.
guidance.qsort_desc: Ensure your grid capacity exactly matches the number of statements. A balanced Q-set usually follows a quasi-normal (bell curve) distribution.

checklist.title: Checklist
checklist.ready: All essential parts are ready. You can now launch your study!
checklist.pending: Complete the required steps above to enable study activation.

unsaved_changes.title: Unsaved changes
unsaved_changes.description: You have unsaved changes in your study design. If you leave now, these changes will be lost.

tips.title: Quick tip
tips.pilot: Always run a test sort yourself before sharing the link with participants.

toolbar.title: Design
toolbar.test_run_help: Preview as a participant. These runs aren't included in your data.

sync.wait_save: Saving in progress... Please wait a moment.
sync.recovery_desc: We found a local version of this study that is more recent than the one on the server. Would you like to restore it?

errors.no_statements: Study must have at least one statement.
errors.capacity_mismatch: Grid capacity ({{total}}) does not match statement count ({{count}}).
errors.no_translations: Study must have at least one language translation.
errors.missing_consent_title: Consent title is missing for language '{{lang}}'.
errors.missing_consent_description: Consent description is missing for language '{{lang}}'.
errors.missing_grid_instructions: Q-Sort instructions are missing for language '{{lang}}'.
errors.missing_step_title: Step {{index}} title is missing for language '{{lang}}'.
errors.empty_statement_text: Statement '{{code}}' has empty text for language '{{lang}}'.
errors.missing_question_label: Question '{{id}}' is missing a label for language '{{lang}}' ({{section}}).
errors.missing_option_label: Option {{index}} of question '{{id}}' is missing a label for language '{{lang}}' ({{section}}).

languages.manage_desc: Enable or disable languages available for participants to see in this study.
languages.auto_translate_desc: Soon you will be able to use AI to automatically translate your study into 30+ languages.
languages.activate_desc: Choose a source language to copy translations from. This prevents empty fields for participants.
languages.copy_notice: All fields will be copied. You will see a visual indicator on fields that still need your translation.

process_steps.title: Study steps overview
process_steps.desc: Define the phases shown to participants on the welcome page. If left empty, default steps will be used.

empty.title: No custom steps defined
empty.desc: The study will use the standard q-methodology workflow (meet, first impressions, perspective, why).

defaults.new_desc: Explain what the participant will do in this phase.

reset.process_steps_confirm: Are you sure you want to reset all process steps to defaults? This will replace your custom steps.

inconsistent.rough_disabled: The 'First impressions' (rough sort) step is in this list but the study has rough sort disabled. Participants will not see it.
inconsistent.presort_disabled: The 'Let's meet' (pre-sort survey) step is in this list but the study has the pre-sort survey disabled. Participants will not see it.

fields.default_lang_help: This language will be shown to participants by default if their browser language is not supported.
fields.title: Study title
fields.subtitle: Subtitle (optional)
fields.objective_placeholder: What is the research question or purpose of this study?
fields.task_placeholder: Describe the study process here (number of steps, estimated duration, etc.). This field is optional if you use the custom steps above.
fields.legal_placeholder: Enter the full legal agreement text...

intro.consent_desc: Participants must agree to these terms before starting.

condition.title: Condition of instruction
condition.label: Define condition of instruction
condition.desc: This is the core prompt that guides participants throughout the sorting process.
condition.placeholder: E.g. Please rank the following statements from those you most agree with to those you most disagree with
condition.grid_desc: This is the core instruction guiding participants during the Q-Sort process.
condition.pre_desc: Instruction given to participants during the initial rough sort.
condition.pre_placeholder: E.g. Based on your personal point of view; divide the cards into three piles...

tips.title: Why is this important?
tips.desc: In q-methodology, the 'condition of instruction' determines the point of view from which the participant sorts the items. It should be clear and consistent across all sorting phases.

questions.enable_presort_desc: Collect demographic information or other data before the sorting task.

empty.title: No questions yet
empty.desc: Click above to add your first question

bulk.title: Bulk editor (quick paste)
bulk.desc: One statement per line. Supports "code: text" and excel copy-paste (multi-language).

set.title: Q-set
set.confirm_clear: Are you sure you want to delete ALL statements? This cannot be undone.
set.confirm_reset_codes: Are you sure you want to re-sequence all statement codes (s1, s2, s3...)?

settings.title: Research settings
settings.desc: Configure how statements are presented to participants
settings.show_codes_desc: Display statement codes (e.g., "S1", "S2") alongside the text.
settings.randomize_desc: Present statements in random order to each participant (consistent within session)

grid.title: Q-Sort distribution grid
grid.desc: The grid shape forces participants to discriminate between statements, approximating a normal distribution.
grid.symmetry_lock_desc: Automatically apply changes to opposite columns to maintain a balanced distribution.
grid.auto_balance_desc: Reshape grid to a balanced semi-normal distribution
grid.reshaped: Grid reshaped to a balanced distribution
grid.no_statements: Add statements first to auto-shape the grid
grid.tooltip_desc: Q-methodology uses a quasi-normal distribution (kurtosis > 0) to ensure participants make meaningful distinctions. The pyramid shape prevents central tendency bias.
grid.locked_desc: Structural changes (statement codes, grid) are disabled because the study has collected data or is no longer in draft mode. You can still edit text translations.
grid.locked_active: This study is active. Switch to draft mode to edit.
grid.locked_paused: This study is paused. Switch to draft mode to edit.
grid.locked_closed: This study is closed. The design is archived.
grid.mismatch_desc: You have {{statements}} statements but the grid only has slots for {{slots}} items. Please adjust the columns below to match.
grid.unlock_hint: Go to data explorer to purge sessions and unlock structure

extreme.title: Targeted columns
extreme.desc: Select columns that trigger follow-up questions.
extreme.prompt_placeholder: Why did you place this statement here?

random_comments.title: Allow random comments
random_comments.desc: Allow participants to add comments to any statement in the grid.

custom.title: Custom questions
custom.desc: Add custom questions to the post-sort survey.

missing.title: Ask about missing statements
missing.desc: If yes, please describe them briefly.
missing.prompt_placeholder: Please feel free to suggest and explain.

email.title: Participant follow-up
email.desc: Collect email addresses for follow-up or research results.
email.results: Offer to subscribe to a mailing list about study outcomes
email.interview_warning: Note: agreeing to be contacted lifts anonymity for the researcher.
email.results_hint: Email solely for results, anonymity preserved.

audio.title: Audio Recording
audio.desc: Allow participants to record audio responses instead of text.
audio.max_duration_help: Maximum recording time per question (30–600 seconds).
audio.participant_choice_info: Participants can respond with text, audio, or both for each question.
audio.storage_unavailable_title: Audio capture is unavailable on this server
audio.storage_unavailable_body: Object storage (S3) is not configured, so audio responses cannot be collected. You can still enable audio below, but participants will answer in text only — no audio is recorded and they see no error. Configure object storage and restart the server to capture audio.

interface.title: Interface customization
interface.desc: Customize the buttons and labels of the interface to match your study's tone (e.g., changing "agree/disagree" to "like/dislike").

terms.title: Sorting terminology
terms.desc: Define the labels for the spontaneous sort and the grid spectrum.

hints.title: Methodology tips
hints.desc: Add or modify floating hints to help participants during the grid sort phase.

help.title: Step guidance (what/why)
help.desc: Customize the context-sensitive help available to participants at each step via the '?' overlay.

accent.title: Accent color
accent.desc: This color will be used for buttons, links, and highlights throughout the study.

logo.title: Study logo
logo.desc: Upload a custom logo to replace the default Open-Q branding.
logo.label: Logo URL
logo.hint: Recommended size: 200x50px. Supports PNG, SVG, JPG.
logo.help_desc: The logo is displayed at the top of every study page (navigation bar) and on the welcome page. It reinforces your project's visual identity.

partners.title: Institutional partners
partners.desc: Add logos of supporting institutions or partners.
partners.help_desc: Partner logos are displayed on the landing page as well as in the study header, next to the main logo.

upload.invalid_type: Invalid file type. Use PNG, JPG, or SVG.
upload.drag_drop: Drag & drop or click to upload

activate_dialog.title: Activate this study?
activate_dialog.description: Activating opens recruitment and unlocks public links. Real participants can begin submitting.
activate_dialog.attestation: I have reviewed the consent text, the retention policy, and confirm this study is ready to receive real participants.

data.title: Data
data.description: Monitor real-time participation, analyze data quality, and manage research sessions.

actions.clear_all_confirm: DANGER: This will permanently delete ALL participant data for this study. This cannot be undone.
actions.clear_all_error: Could not clear data. Check your permissions and try again.

confirm_discard.title: Discard participant?
confirm_discard.description: This participant will be excluded from exports and analysis. You can restore them later.
confirm_discard.reason_placeholder: Why is this participant being discarded?

confirm_restore.title: Restore participant?
confirm_restore.description: This participant will be included in exports and analysis again.

csv.title: Universal CSV
csv.desc: Raw rectangular data. Best for r, python, SPSS, or excel analysis. Includes all metadata.

json.title: KenQ JSON
json.desc: Optimized for the web-kenq analysis tool. Contains study definition and sorts.

zip.title: PQMethod bundle (ZIP)
zip.desc: Contains legacy .DAT and .STA files for DOS PQMethod analysis.

table.duplicate_ip_hint: Shares IP hash with other participants

toast.error: Could not update participant. Check your connection and try again.

survey.title: Questionnaire responses
survey.csv_note: Full responses available in CSV export.

search.placeholder: Search by ID or recruitment token...

errors.load_failed: Cloud sync interrupted. Please check your connection.

empty.no_participants_desc: Share your study link to start collecting responses.
empty.contract_body: Submissions and exports will appear here. Share the study link to start collecting.

team.title: Study team
team.description: Manage collaborators and study-level access control.
team.invite_description: Generate a secure link to invite a new member to this study.
team.share_label: Share this link with the invitee:

permissions.owner: Can delete the study and manage team.
permissions.editor: Can modify design and view results.

participants.title: Ready to launch?
participants.desc: Share your study link to start collecting responses. Your first participant is just a click away.

team.title: You're flying solo
team.desc: Invite collaborators to help manage this study. They can view data, edit the design, or just observe.

designer.title: Tabula rasa
designer.desc: This study has no statements yet. Add your Q-set items to begin designing your sort.

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Data retention, archiving, and study deletion.

lifecycle.title: Archiving
lifecycle.description: Archive the study to make it read-only for everyone.
lifecycle.notice: To archive a study, it must first be closed. Once archived, no further changes can be made.

danger.title: Danger zone
danger.notice: Deleting a study removes ALL data (participants, answers, config) permanently. Study must be archived before deletion.
danger.delete_confirm: Are you sure? This action is IRREVERSIBLE.
danger.delete_dialog_intro: Permanently deletes sorts, audio, and analysis runs.
danger.delete_dialog_typed_label: Type the study slug to confirm

settings.save_error: Could not save settings. Verify the values and try again.
settings.save_error_desc: Failed to update settings. Slug might be taken.
settings.archive_success_desc: The study is now archived and read-only.
settings.archive_error_desc: Failed to archive study. Is it closed?
settings.delete_success_desc: The study has been permanently removed.
settings.delete_error: Could not delete study. Make sure all data has been cleared first.

storage.title: Audio Storage Usage
storage.quota_help: Maximum total audio storage for this study (10–1000 MB).
storage.warning_high_usage: Storage usage is high. Consider increasing the quota or removing old recordings.
storage.save_error: Could not update storage quota. Try again.

retention.title: Data retention
retention.help: Common values: 6, 12, 24, 60 months. Leave empty for system default (12).
retention.save_error: Failed to update data retention policy
retention.range_error: Retention must be an integer between 1 and 240 months.

privacy.title: Data privacy
privacy.bulk_desc: Remove personal data from completed participants submitted before a cutoff date. Q-sort rankings are preserved.
privacy.cutoff_help: Only completed participants submitted strictly before this date will be anonymised.
privacy.preview_zero: No participants qualify for this cutoff. Try an earlier date.
privacy.confirm_candidates: {{count}} completed participant(s) submitted before {{date}} will be anonymised.
privacy.confirm_preserved: Q-sort rankings are kept; identity is removed.
privacy.confirm_irreversible: This action is immediate and cannot be undone.
privacy.cutoff_from_policy: Default derived from study retention policy ({{months}} months).

empty.title: No participant data yet
empty.body: This page activates once participants submit. Share the study link first.

consent.title: Consent form
consent.description: What participants see before they enter the study. Edited in Study design.

settings.title: Project settings

general.title: General settings
general.desc: Identity and primary identification of your project.
general.slug_hint: Changing the slug will update all your research dashboard links.

settings.team_growth_desc: Need more researchers? You can invite collaborators to your project. They will be able to manage all studies within this project.

team.title: Team members
team.desc: List of users with access to this project.
team.remove_confirm: Are you sure you want to remove this member?
team.remove_dialog_body: {{name}} will lose access to this project. They can be re-invited later.
team.growth_desc: Need more researchers? You can invite collaborators to your project. They will be able to manage all studies within this project.

admin.label: Admin
admin.desc: Can manage project settings, members, and all studies.

owner.label: Owner
owner.desc: Full project, member, and study control.

member.label: Member
member.desc: Can create and manage all studies. Restricted from project settings.

viewer.label: Viewer
viewer.desc: Read-only access to all studies and data in the project.

members.quota_full_tooltip: Member limit reached. Increase MAX_MEMBERS_PER_PROJECT or remove a member.

members.title: Team members
members.description: Manage who can access this project and what they can do.

participants.title: Participants
participants.description: Manage participants and explore submitted data.

grid.select_instruction: Select a card on the grid to view details.

survey.no_answers: No answers recorded for this section.

users.title: Users
users.subtitle: Manage platform accounts and audit hygiene.
users.generic_error: Something went wrong. Please try again.
users.load_error_body: The user list could not be retrieved. Please refresh the page or try again later.

recovery_link.title: Password-reset link
recovery_link.help: Share this single-use link with the user out of band. It expires per the password-reset window.

set_email_dialog.title: Set user email
set_email_dialog.description: This logs {{email}} out; they must sign in again using the new address. No confirmation email is sent.
set_email_dialog.label: New email address

confirm.delete_body: Permanently delete {{email}}. This cannot be undone.
confirm.reset-totp_body: Disable 2FA for {{email}}. They will need to set it up again.
confirm.force-password-reset_body: Invalidate the password for {{email}} and require a reset on next login.
confirm.deactivate_body: Immediately log {{email}} out and block sign-in until reactivated.
confirm.promote_body: Grant {{email}} full superuser privileges over the platform.
